title: Frontier justice: navigating the future legal landscape for private actors in space law

authors.name: Tatiana Sainati, Christina Lucas and Natalia Szlarb

summary:
At the dawn of the Space Age, then President-elect John F. Kennedy spoke to the American people of “a new frontier” of unknown opportunities and perils, unfulfilled hopes and unfilled threats, uncharted science and unsolved problems. Six years later, Star Trek expanded on President Kennedy’s new frontier premise with tales of the starship Enterprise boldly […]

title: China signals new target for 2027 asteroid deflection test
summary:
China has identified a new target near-Earth asteroid for its first planetary defense kinetic test mission, which is scheduled to launch in December 2027. 
The post China signals new target for 2027 asteroid deflection test appeared first on SpaceNews.

title: NASA grappling with planetary science funding shortfall
summary:
NASA’s planetary science program, while spared steep cuts proposed last year, is still facing a funding shortfall that requires “strategic choices” about which missions to continue.

title: OHB Sweden to build Sterna weather constellation
summary: Thanks to the success of the Arctic Weather Satellite prototype and Eumetsat’s recent greenlight to develop a full constellation of similar satellites called Sterna, the European Space Agency has awarded OHB Sweden with the contract to build 20 satellites.

title: Wave of Dust Rolls Through Texas
summary: An advancing cold front kicked up a sharp line of sand and other small particles that swept over the high plains.

title: Space Force overhauls buying structure with new mission portfolios
summary:
New structure gives ‘Portfolio Acquisition Executives’ authority over funding, requirements and integration across major space missions

title: Telesat pivots Lightspeed toward defense as delays push global service to 2028
summary:
Telesat plans to carve out 25% of its Lightspeed broadband constellation for military Ka-band as the program’s latest delay pushes global service into early 2028, creating more room to align the design with shifting geopolitical priorities.

title: NASA’s X-59 Prepares for Second Flight
summary: NASA’s X-59 experimental aircraft is preparing for its second flight, a step that will set the pace for more flight testing in 2026.  Over the coming months, NASA will take the quiet supersonic jet faster and higher, while validating safety and performance, a process known as envelope expansion.  NASA test pilot Jim “Clue” Less will be at the X-59’s […]

title: Office of Space Commerce weighing options for TraCSS user fees
summary:
The Trump administration has not yet decided whether to charge for space safety data despite a change in space policy enabling the government to do so.
